records.ety: [OE. sengen, AS. sengan in besengan (akin to D. zengen, G. sengen), originally, to cause to sing, fr. AS. singan to sing, in allusion to the singing or hissing sound often produced when a substance is singed, or slightly burned. See Sing.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Singed (snjd); p. pr. & vb. n. Singeing (snj"ng).]

defs: To burn slightly or superficially; to burn the surface of; to burn the ends or outside of; as, to singe the hair or the skin.
quotes: You sulphurous and thought-executing fires, . . . Singe my white head!
I singed the toes of an ape through a burning glass.

defs: To remove the nap of (cloth), by passing it rapidly over a red-hot bar, or over a flame, preliminary to dyeing it.
To remove the hair or down from (a plucked chicken or the like) by passing it over a flame.
A burning of the surface; a slight burn.
